channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: I Got Tired of Re-Explaining My Codebase to Claude Every Day - So I Built ContextKeep
item.description: The Problem: AI Amnesia If you use AI coding assistants like Claude, GPT-4, or Cursor, you've probably experienced this cycle: Start a coding session, explain your project context Make decisions,...

item.title: Women and Gaming: The 52/48 Split, the Toxicity Problem, and the Influencers Changing Everything
item.description: Women and Gaming: The 52/48 Split, the Toxicity Problem, and the Influencers Changing Everything By Krishna Soni | The Power of Gaming Here is a number that should have changed everything: 48%....

item.title: Gaming and the Aging Brain: How Playing Video Games Could Delay Dementia by a Decade
item.description: Gaming and the Aging Brain: How Playing Video Games Could Delay Dementia by a Decade My grandfather was eighty-one when he picked up his first controller. He had been a crossword man his whole...

item.title: 🚀 I Built an AI Tool That Automatically Replies to Emails & Handles Follow-ups
item.description: 🤖 What Replyzen AI Does Replyzen is a micro-SaaS platform designed to automate email conversations using AI. It helps businesses: • Automatically respond to incoming emails • Generate intelligent...

item.title: I built a discovery site for everything Google and social media overlook
item.description: I built Internet Intro, a portal to the independent web (beta) I built Internet Intro to surface the cool, handmade, experimental corners of the web that search engines and social feeds usually...

item.title: I built an AI pipeline to give my partner her time back (and accidentally cured my engineering burnout).
item.description: After months of dealing with burnout, I let the "builder" in me go dormant. But recently, I found the perfect excuse to start building again: my partner's part-time job. As an assistant, she spent...

item.title: The FDA Approved a Video Game as Medicine: What Comes Next
item.description: The FDA Approved a Video Game as Medicine: What Comes Next There was a time when a doctor prescribing a video game would have been the punchline of a joke. Parents were told to pry controllers...

item.title: Don't You Dare Go Hollow: The Dark Souls Philosophy for Living Your One-Playthrough Life
item.description: Don't You Dare Go Hollow: The Dark Souls Philosophy for Living Your One-Playthrough Life There is a line in Dark Souls that has no business being as profound as it is. You are about to walk into a...

item.title: Like LEGO? Love Python! 🧱🐍 Ep.5
item.description: Episode 5: The LEGO Instruction Manual (Abstraction) Welcome Back, Master Builder! 📋 Remember those iconic LEGO instruction manuals? You know, the ones that show you WHAT the final creation should...

item.title: The Gilded Rose Kata: Composition Over Inheritance
item.description: The Gilded Rose refactoring kata is a classic coding exercise that challenges developers to refactor legacy code while adding new functionality. Most solutions reach for inheritance as the primary...

item.title: ‘Not built right the first time’ — Musk’s xAI is starting over again, again
item.description: The AI lab is revamping its effort to build an AI coding tool, with two new executives joining from Cursor.

item.title: Lawyer behind AI psychosis cases warns of mass casualty risks
item.description: AI chatbots have been linked to suicides for years. Now one lawyer says they are showing up in mass casualty cases too, and the technology is moving faster than the safeguards.

item.title: States’ anti-monopoly case against Live Nation continues Monday
item.description: The Live Nation-Ticketmaster trial is back on. Dozens of states are expected to move forward with their claims against the company's alleged concert industry monopoly beginning on Monday,...

item.title: Spotify tests letting users directly customize their Taste Profile
item.description: Spotify Premium users in New Zealand will be the first to experience the service's latest personalization feature. The company is letting users view and make changes to their Taste Profile...

item.title: Nyne, founded by a father-son duo, gives AI agents the human context they’re missing
item.description: The data infrastructure startup raised $5.3 million in seed funding led by Wischoff Ventures and South Park Commons.

item.title: Friday Squid Blogging: Increased Squid Population in the Falklands
item.description: Some good news: squid stocks seem to be recovering in the waters off the Falkland Islands. As usual, you can also use this squid post to talk about the security stories in the news that I haven’t...

item.title: Microsoft’s Copilot AI assistant is coming to current-gen Xbox consoles this year
item.description: Xbox is getting ready to launch its Gaming Copilot AI assistant on "current-generation consoles" this year, according to a report from GamesRadar. Sonali Yadav, Xbox's product manager for gaming...

item.title: Trump Mobile is just one in the crowd of conservative carriers
item.description: Where's the Trump phone? We're going to keep talking about it every week. This week, I wanted to see how Trump Mobile stacks up to its conservative carrier competition. Trump Mobile isn't unique....

item.title: Digg’s open beta shuts down after just two months, blaming AI bot spam
item.description: It's only been a year since Digg founder Kevin Rose, Reddit cofounder Alexis Ohanian, and a few others announced the link-sharing site would relaunch, promising a "social discovery built by...

item.title: Supply-chain attack using invisible code hits GitHub and other repositories
item.description: Unicode that's invisible to the human eye was largely abandoned—until attackers took notice.

item.title: Beyond Semantic Similarity: Introducing NVIDIA NeMo Retriever’s Generalizable Agentic Retrieval Pipeline
Steven Spielberg says he’s ‘never used AI’ in any of his films
item.description: At SXSW, Steven Spielberg said AI has uses in many fields, but not when it comes to replacing creative people in film and TV writing.

item.title: Instagram is getting rid of end-to-end encrypted DMs that ‘very few’ people used
item.description: Instagram will no longer support end-to-end encrypted messages starting May 8th. In a statement to The Verge, Meta spokesperson Dina El-Kassaby Luce says the platform is discontinuing the feature...

item.title: The biggest AI stories of the year (so far)
item.description: The AI industry is constantly churning out news, like major acquisitions, indie developer successes, public outcry, and existentially dangerous contract negotiations.

item.title: Google Pixel 10A review: Just buy the 9A
item.description: I'm not entirely sure why the Pixel 10A exists. Google hasn't upgraded the chipset, cameras, or battery in the new phone, and the tweaks it's made elsewhere are minimal at best. The flatter camera...

item.title: Backbone’s versatile pro controller is nearly matching its best price to date
item.description: Mobile gaming has come a long way over the course of the last decade or so, but we all know that smartphones simply can’t match the visceral, tactile feel you get while playing with a dedicated...

item.title: Adobe will pay $75 million to settle US cancellation fee lawsuit
item.description: Adobe says it will pay $75 million to resolve a lawsuit filed by the US government alleging that the creative software giant harmed consumers by making its subscriptions intentionally hard to...

item.title: Chinese Hackers Target Southeast Asian Militaries with AppleChris and MemFun Malware
item.description: A suspected China-based cyber espionage operation has targeted Southeast Asian military organizations as part of a state-sponsored campaign that dates back to at least 2020. Palo Alto Networks...

item.title: The wild six weeks for NanoClaw’s creator that led to a deal with Docker
item.description: Gavriel Cohen is living an open source developer's dream as his project has achieved acclaim and a partnership with Docker in a matter of weeks.

item.title: Meta to Shut Down Instagram End-to-End Encrypted Chat Support Starting May 2026
item.description: Meta has announced plans to discontinue support for end-to-end encryption (E2EE) for chats on Instagram after May 8, 2026. "If you have chats that are impacted by this change, you will see...
